rationale: The cited paper is topically related (replacement/upgrade decisions in autos and consumer response to new-product purchase contexts), so it could plausibly be part of the theoretical background rather than a purely incidental citation. However, the provided evidence includes only a DOI match with no in-text citation context, and the citing paper’s stated mechanism (perceived brand-relationship distance) does not clearly indicate substantive use of the cited paper’s trade-in mechanism, so a safe keep/drop call is not possible.
